title: Elsinoré den Tasia

{{Quote|At approximately 3900 BBY, Queen Tasia of Grizmallt sponsored one of the last of her world's colonization missions.|A New Republic historical document by Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
[[Queen/Legends|Queen]] '''Elsinoré den Tasia''' ruled the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]] beginning around [[3950 BBY]]. [[Gender/Legends|She]] sponsored numerous [[Colonist/Legends|colonization]] expeditions throughout her reign, including one that led to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] occupation of the [[Renatasia system/Legends|Renatasia system]]. Many of the expeditions failed, and around [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]], Tasia sponsored the last of those expeditions, which was carried out by three [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ships]]; the ''[[Beneficent Tasia]]'', ''[[Constant]]'', and ''[[Mother Vima]]''. Tasia [[Death/Legends|died]] shortly afterward, wishing the [[Starship/Legends|ships]] would find a home in her final moments. The ships would later land on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], which the colonists inhabited, becoming known as the [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]]. Some purported descendants of the colonists wore a style of [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] that acknowledged a connection to Tasia thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later.

==Biography==
===Queen of Grizmallt===
{{Quote|They are traditionalists—tonsorially as well as politically. The style of dress and facial adornments pay homage to the regalia of Queen Elsinore ''[sic]'' den Tasia of Grizmallt, who dispatched an expeditionary fleet of humans to the planet some four thousand years ago, and to whom some Naboo claim to be able to trace an unbroken ancestry.|Larsh Hill, about the Naboo|audio=Tonsorially.flac|<ref name="Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> }}
[[File:Renatasia_system.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Elsinoré den Tasia sponsored numerous colonial missions, including the Human colonization of the Renatasia system.]]
Elsinoré den Tasia was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] who ascended as [[Queen/Legends|queen]] of<ref name="GB" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]] around [[3950 BBY]]. At the beginning of her reign, Tasia ushered in a new age of exploration and [[Colonist/Legends|colonization]],<ref name="GB" /> sponsoring fourteen colonies in 3950 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> including the [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonies on the eight planets of the [[Renatasia system/Legends|Renatasia system]],<ref name="Renatasia">{{CSWECite|III|92|Renatasia system}}</ref> which became isolated from the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TEA" /> In the following [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]], Grizmallt launched hundreds of expeditions into unexplored areas of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] beyond known [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]], although many of the [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ships]] were destroyed or lost.<ref name="GB" /> In approximately [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> in the midst of a [[Grizmallti civil war/Legends|civil war]] on Grizmallt,<ref name="Nexus">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref> the [[Death/Legends|dying]] Tasia sponsored the last such expedition, which consisted of three<ref name="GB" /> colony ships—<ref name="TEA" /> ''[[Beneficent Tasia]]'', ''[[Constant]]'', and ''[[Mother Vima]]''—<ref name="GB" /> carrying Human [[Grizmallti/Legends|Grizmallti]] refugees from the war.<ref name="Nexus" />

The expedition was blessed by a famous [[Unidentified famous Jedi Master|Jedi Master]],<ref name="GB" /> and led by the [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]],<ref name="Secrets">''[[Secrets of Naboo]]''</ref> with the passengers of the [[Starship/Legends|starships]] seeking fortune and glory away from mainstream galactic civilization. The three colony ships were headed for the dangerous southern quadrant of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], and Tasia's dying wish was that the expedition would find a habitable world. After her death, Grizmallt lost contact with the ''Beneficient Tasia'', and the expedition was assumed to have failed. However, the expedition eventually reached the [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="GB" /> in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]],<ref name="TEA" /> as if guided by Tasia's last wish.<ref name="GB" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|There, the Royal Script still graces the Palazzo Reina and other buildings dating to the rule of Queen Tasia, and is used on street signs in the capital's Old Quarter.|Doctor Milanda Vorgan, writing about Grizmallt in "The Written Word: A Brief Introduction to the Writing Systems of Galactic Basic"|<ref name="Written">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref>}}
[[File:Naboo_TEA.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Tasia's sponsorship led to the Human colonization of Naboo (pictured)]]
One of the ships crashed on Naboo,<ref name="Crisis">{{SW|url=explore/the-clone-wars/ep418|text="Crisis on Naboo" - ''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide|nolive=1}}"</ref> while the others landed safely. The Humans carried by the ships found Naboo to be a pleasant pastoral world. They colonized the planet, and the descendants of the colonists became the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]],<ref name="GB" /> although the Grizmallti would not learn their fate for some [[Time/Legends|time]] afterward.<ref name="NEC" /> Some members of the Naboo claimed to be able to trace an unbroken ancestry to Tasia almost 4,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later. The [[Clothing/Legends|dress]] style and facial adornments of some Naboo paid homage to Queen Tasia, a fact acknowledged by the [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] financier [[Larsh Hill]] in a meeting with the Naboo [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ars Veruna/Legends|Ars Veruna]] between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]].<ref name="Plagueis" />

The lost Human colonies in Renatasia were ultimately rediscovered in [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]]. Tasia's role in the colonization of Naboo was mentioned in a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|historical document]] written in [[36 ABY]] by [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]],<ref name="NEC" /> as well as in the academic text "[[The Written Word: A Brief Introduction to the Writing Systems of Galactic Basic]]" in [[38 ABY]] by [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Milanda Vorgan]]. The text acknowledged that the [[Palazzo Reina]] and other buildings from Tasia's reign still stood, bearing the [[Royal Script of Grizmallt]].<ref name="Written" />

==Personality and traits==
Queen Tasia financially supported multiple colonial endeavors throughout her life and wished for the safe landing of the last expedition in her final moments.<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|And, given the vaguely old-world European culture of Naboo, it's possible that a little Spanish infuence could crop up in naming conventions, making it possible to refer to Queen Tasia as 'Reina Tasia.' |Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|34|Lostwords: The Essential Guide to Episode I}}</ref>}}
[[File:GalacticBattlegrounds.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Elsinoré den Tasia was mentioned in ''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'' after being developed for the canceled reference book ''The Essential Guide to Episode I''.]]
Elsinoré den Tasia was originally created by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[W. Haden Blackman]] for ''[[The Essential Guide to Episode I]]'', a reference book that was ultimately canceled. Blackman later included information about Tasia in the DataBank entry for the Naboo<ref name="Blog" /> in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.<ref name="GB" /> Wallace named the character as an [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter egg]] to the<ref name="Blog" /> [[1983]] novel ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' by [[L. Neil Smith]], which mentions the lost colonies in the Renatasia system.<ref name="Lando">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]''</ref> Taking influence from the vaguely [[Wikipedia:European|European]] culture of Naboo, Wallace considered that a [[Wikipedia:Spanish language|Spanish]] influence could appear in the naming conventions, combining the Spanish word for queen, "reina," with Tasia's surname, becoming "Reina Tasia." Wallace hoped that fans would appreciate the [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]].<ref name="Blog" />

When writing ''[[The Written Word]]'', an article for the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] subscription service on [[StarWars.com]], author [[John Hazlett]] acknowledged Wallace's earlier backstory by mentioning the Palazzo Reina, which could be roughly translated as "Queen's Palace."<ref name="Word endnotes">{{Blog|JSarek|8|Endnotes for The Written Word}}</ref> Tasia's sponsoring of the Renatasia colony would later become [[canon]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', written by Wallace and [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="TEA" /> Her name was misspelled as "Elsinore den Tasia" in the [[2012]] novel [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] by [[James Luceno]].<ref name="Plagueis" />
